title: Asia Centric: Asia’s Tech Giants Are Now Challenging the Mag 7
summary: Earnings season is officially underway and Asia’s technology giants are setting the early pace. TSMC, Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix have all delivered strong numbers, prompting analysts to significantly raise earnings expectations for South Korea and Taiwan – two of the world’s best-performing stock markets.

title: In Tariff Refund Process, First Payment Set to Go Out May 11
summary: The Trump administration said the first tariff refund payment is set to go out around May 11, even as thousands of US importers encounter issues with a new online portal designed to handle claims on the $166 billion in duties that were overturned by the Supreme Court.

title: Starmer’s Post-Election Survival Strategy Takes Shape as the Iran War Goes Long
summary: Starmer’s post-election survival strategy takes shape as the Iran war goes on longer than expected.

title: Treasuries Slump as Fed Dissents Spur Wagers on 2027 Rate Hike
summary: Treasuries slumped, with two-year yields rising the most on a Federal Reserve decision day since 2022, as traders boosted bets the central bank may need to raise interest rates amid persistent inflation pressures.

title: Yen Falls Below 160 Per Dollar to Weakest Mark Since 2024
summary: The yen extended its slide beyond 160 per dollar to its weakest mark this year, fueling risk that Japanese officials may step into the market to offer support.

title: Ford CFO on Q1 Earnings, Outlook and Impact of Gas Prices
summary: Ford CFO Sherry House discusses first-quarter earnings, SUV and pick-up truck sales amid higher gas prices and the automaker's full-year outlook on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: India’s Homegrown $1 Billion High-Speed Trading Unicorn Goes Global
summary: Graviton is expanding in Singapore and London as competition and regulatory hurdles build in its home market.

title: Mamdani vs. Griffin Standoff Puts $6 Billion Tower at Risk
summary: Citadel’s Ken Griffin said he’s meeting with New York Governor Kathy Hochul on Thursday to talk about the future direction of America’s biggest city after a spat with Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s over Griffin's $238 million Manhattan penthouse. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Treasury Yields Climb as Fed Split Amid War Risks: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street traders drove bonds lower and stocks wavered as a divided Federal Reserve held rates steady and signaled the war in Iran is clouding the economic outlook. Brent crude hit the highest since 2022.

title: Divided Fed Likely to Continue, BlackRock's Rosenberg Says
summary: Jeffrey Rosenberg, portfolio manager of the systematic multi-strategy fund at BlackRock, says the market took the Federal Reserve easing bias out at the onset of the war in Iran. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: When Will Jerome Powell Leave the Fed?
summary: Fed Chair Jerome Powell said he intends to remain at the central bank as a member of its Board of Governors, but it's not clear when he'll leave completely. Jim Bianco of Bianco Research says Powell could stick around as a disruptor. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The Deal with Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly | Season 4 Trailer
summary: The Deal with Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly showcases intimate conversations with business titans and sports champions that reveal their investment philosophies, missed opportunities and more.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Stocks Mixed as Oil Jumps, Fed Holds Rates Before Earnings
summary: US equities closed mixed amid volatile trading on Wednesday as oil prices surged and a standoff between the US and Iran over the Strait of Hormuz escalated. Investors also assessed a split policy decision by the Federal Reserve while awaiting earnings from four megacap companies that were released after the bell.

title: BP and Venezuela Sign Pact to Explore for Offshore Gas
summary: BP Plc and Venezuela agreed on a deal to explore for natural gas offshore as the South American country’s energy revival gathers pace following the US capture of Nicolas Maduro.

title: Bloomberg Deals 4/29/2026
summary: A weekly, midday program that delivers high-impact, editorially driven coverage of the most important corporate transactions shaping the global market. Today's guests: Pershing Square CEO & Founder Bill Ackman, LionTree Advisors Managing Director Ariel Granoff,  Cain Co-Founder & CEO Jonathan Goldstein, Boston Consulting Managing Director & Partner Travel, Transport & Leisure Tom McCaleb, and AstraZeneca CFO Aradhana Sarin. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: War, Fuel Prices Seen Steering Travel Industry Toward M&A
summary: War in Iran and higher oil prices can be expected to keep tourists closer to home, disrupting the travel and hospitality industry and potentially spurring dealmaking.

title: Mamdani vs. Griffin Standoff Puts $6 Million Tower at Risk
summary: Citadel’s Ken Griffin said he’s meeting with New York Governor Kathy Hochul on Thursday to talk about the future direction of America’s biggest city after a spat with Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s over Griffin's $238 million Manhattan penthouse (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Charges Toward $120 With No End in Sight to US-Iran War
summary: Oil surged to the highest since June 2022, with no end in sight to the US-Iran conflict or choked-off energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz, heightening concerns over a rapidly shrinking global supply cushion.

title: Opportunity in Small, Midcap Travel M&A: LionTree
summary: Ariel Granoff, managing director of LionTree Advisors, joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals."  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Need To Invest in Distinctive Travel Brands: Cain CEO
summary: Jonathan Goldstein, co-founder & CEO of Cain International, discusses the importance of focusing on both brand strength and operational excellence when investing in luxury assets with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals."  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Significant Airline M&A on the Horizon: McCaleb
summary: Tom McCaleb, managing director & partner of travel, transport, & leisure at Boston Consulting Group Transport, joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals."  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Powell Says He Has 'No Choice' But to Stay at Fed
summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell says concerns about recent legal attacks on the central bank has led him to the decision to stay at the institution and will leave when it is appropriate to do so. While Powell’s term as Fed chair ends on May 15, his seat on the Board of Governors doesn’t expire until 2028. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Falls for Third Day as Iran War Heightens Inflation Risks
summary: Gold extended a decline with the indefinite closure of the Strait of Hormuz continuing to heighten inflation risks and the Federal Reserve signaling the war in Iran is clouding the economic outlook.

title: Trump Argues UAE’s OPEC Exit Could Lower Energy Prices Amid War
summary: US President Donald Trump said the United Arab Emirates’ decision to leave OPEC would help lower energy prices, which have spiked due to the war in Iran and continue to climb.

title: Copper Slips as Traders Weigh Iran War Impact, China Demand
summary: Copper edged lower as investors weighed the ongoing risks that the Iran war poses to global growth against the outlook for demand in top buyer China.

title: JetBlue, Booking Give Mixed Update on Travel Demand
summary: Bloomberg's Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals" discusses travel demand as fuel prices soar amid the Iran War. This week JetBlue, Booking Holdings, and Hilton gave mixed commentary on future demand for travel.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fed Moving Towards Symmetrical Policy, JPMorgan's Michele Says
summary: "The Fed would be nuts not to move to a symmetrical posture," Bob Michele, global head of fixed income at JPMorgan Asset Management, says on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Sun Pharma, Lilly Lead Latest Healthcare Deal Rush
summary: Bloomberg's Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals" wraps up recent pharma dealmaking. Eli Lilly agreed to buy closely held Ajax Therapeutics for as much as $2.3 billion in cash. Sun Pharmaceutical Industries has agreed to acquire New York-listed women’s health-care company Organon. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fed Can Stay on Hold This Year, JPMorgan's Michele Says
summary: Bob Michele, global head of fixed income at JPMorgan Asset Management, says the inflation risk posed by the Iran conflict means the Federal Reserve can stay on hold through year-end on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fed Leaves Rates Unchanged, Four Officials Dissent
summary: The Federal Reserve leaves interest rates unchanged in a range of 3.5% to 3.75%. But four officials voted against the decision. It’s the first time since 1992 that there were four dissents. Bloomberg’s Michael McKee reports.

title: Iran Having a Nuclear Weapon is a 'Real Danger To The World' Says Sen. Moore Capito
summary:
Republican Senator Shelley Moore Capito joined Balance of Power to discuss the tension in around talks in the Middle East as well as looming FISA expiration. Sen. Moore Capito said the section of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act that is about to expire is an essential tool for the US to investigate and head off foreign threats.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Kone Agrees to Buy TK Elevator for $34.4B
summary: Bloomberg's Liana Baker joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." Kone agreed to acquire TK Elevator for $34.4 billion including debt, transforming the Finnish company into the industry’s dominant player and putting new pressure on its rivals. (Source: Bloomberg)
